steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents North American and international studies (including Finland) showing a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. The Brazil study is the outlier — few studies matched its result. The question asks what conclusion is best supported by that distribution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the hypothesis that the positive association is particular to North America is correct.’: contradicted by Finland's findings confirming the same pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘there were circumstances unique to Pyhtilä and colleagues' study that impeded accurate measurements of mercury levels.’: Pyhtilä's study actually confirmed the positive association — no measurement issues mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water.’: when most studies show a pattern and few show the opposite, the pattern is typical. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water are both much higher in Brazil than elsewhere.’: the passage focuses on correlation, not absolute levels. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
